headline: Sabah and Sarawak Demand Control Over Own Education Systems
context: East Malaysian state governments formally requested devolution of education authority under the Malaysia Agreement 1963.

cards.big: Sabah and Sarawak cite MA63 to demand education devolution from Putrajaya
cards.sub: Federal control over curriculum teacher posting and funding faces legal challenge

cards.big: Malaysia Agreement 1963 Section 18 preserves Borneo states education policy rights
cards.sub: Federal government has progressively centralized control since 1976 Education Act

cards.big: Sarawak has 1,250 schools but only 8% of education budget allocation decisions
cards.sub: State government supplements federal funding with RM450 million annually

cards.big: Scotland manages its own education system separately from England under devolution
cards.sub: Scottish curriculum and qualification systems operate independently since 1999

cards.big: Educational federalism allows local solutions for local conditions rather than Putrajaya one-size-fits-all
Should Sabah and Sarawak have full autonomy over their education systems
